briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 11, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Direct Impacts of Climate Change and Increasing Threats
sections.content: Today's briefing begins with concerns about the direct impacts and escalating threats of climate change intensifying globally. The U.S. ski industry has been directly hit by snowless winters [1], and the New Hampshire ski industry is also expressing deep concerns about climate change [13]. Beyond these economic impacts, climate change is becoming a major cause of global political instability [17], and new approaches are urgently required to protect agriculture in Bangladesh's Haor region [18]. Furthermore, NIO research findings indicate that climate change is linked to increased underwater volcanic activity, adding a warning about unexpected and complex threats [40].

sections.title: ⚠️ Climate Change Outpacing Evolution and the Search for DNA-Based Solutions
sections.content: Next, we must pay attention to a significant warning from the scientific community. According to numerous international news reports, the current pace of climate change is outpacing the evolutionary speed of organisms, portending severe impacts across entire ecosystems [15, 16, 19-38, 41-44, 46-51]. In this critical situation, scientists are exploring ways to use DNA to help organisms adapt to changing environments [15, 19, 27, 33, 37, 42, 47]. Domestically, the possibility that DNA could emerge as a solution to the climate crisis has also been raised [2]. This underscores the importance of scientific and innovative approaches to climate change.

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Initiatives and Renewable Energy Expansion Efforts
sections.content: On a positive note, concrete efforts to achieve carbon neutrality and expand renewable energy are continuing both domestically and internationally. Domestically, Goyang Carbon Zero Forest and SI Solution Co., Ltd. have signed a business agreement for cooperation in responding to climate crises and disasters [4], and Jeju City has finalized the 'Top 5 Carbon Neutrality Action Tasks' directly selected by citizens, encouraging community participation [6]. Furthermore, Pohang City's Climate Crisis Response Committee is reviewing the progress of carbon neutrality initiatives and accelerating policy implementation [8], and the Ministry of Climate is strengthening substantial financial support, including increasing the supplementary budget by 616.2 billion KRW for renewable energy-centered energy transition and support for vulnerable groups [10]. The awarding of the Dongtap Industrial Medal to Seo Seong-jae, Vice President of Technology and Safety at Korea Southern Power, in recognition of his achievements in the renewable energy sector, demonstrates the fruits of these efforts [12].

sections.title: 🌍 Global Climate Action and Promoting Future Generation Participation
sections.content: Internationally, various initiatives to address climate change and movements to encourage future generation participation are also active. In the UK, innovative technologies are paving new ways in renewable energy storage [11], and Canadian airports are making concerted adaptation efforts in response to climate change [14]. Amidst the shared understanding that carbon neutrality is a comprehensive art requiring the harmony of technology and finance [5, 9], Geoje City is raising awareness of carbon neutrality practices among future generations through outreach environmental education [7], and a watershed organization has begun efforts to inspire climate change action among youth using online games [39]. These multifaceted approaches emphasize the importance of global solidarity and future generation education for overcoming the climate crisis.

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, climate change presents complex challenges directly linked to human survival, but at the same time, it also conveys a message of hope that these can be overcome through scientific innovation and global cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.
